response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced engineer with genuine language-tooling interest (built a test runner and assertion library for D, worked on the LabWindows/CVI IDE at National Instruments), but the core required stack is largely absent: no demonstrated Kotlin experience, no Spring or enterprise Java framework work, and Java appears only briefly and peripherally. Recent experience is TypeScript/Node full-stack and DLang, and the IDE work is a decade old and in C/C++ rather than JVM tooling. Strong craftsmanship signals do not offset the missing must-have qualifications for a JVM-focused IDE role.
strengths: Direct IDE development experience (NI LabWindows/CVI) plus authored developer tooling (Trial test runner, Fluent-Asserts)
Strong software craftsmanship background: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, teaching and workshops
Proven ability to work across complex, high-throughput systems (low-latency microservices at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
concerns: No Kotlin and no Spring/enterprise Java framework experience — core required qualifications
Java exposure is minimal and dated; recent work is TypeScript/Node and DLang, not JVM
IDE internals experience is from 2014–2015 and in C/C++, not JVM language tooling
